subject: mlx-community/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-4bit (real model, live)
headline: Real-model A/B/C ablation of the proactive verifiability gate on the 70B. The gate's knowability-tracking is causal not surface (arm B: world-model 1.0 vs surface 0.0), BUT its real-model fabrication-reduction is MODEST (arm A: raw 70B already abstains on 62.5% of unknowable traps; gate delta +0.375 @ cost 0, BELOW the 0.8 offline bar) — the synthetic OFF=1.0 baseline overstated the gain. Scope-bounded to known knowability families (arm C). canClaimAGI false; small-n demonstration.

armA_replication_pack_realModel.finding: the capable 70B self-abstains on 62.5% of unknowable traps, so the proactive gate's incremental fabrication-reduction is +0.375 (not the +1.0 the synthetic ablate_verifiability OFF baseline assumed). Real but modest; below the 0.8 offline delta bar. n=8 traps (small demonstration, not powered).

armB_counterfactual_traps.finding: the gate's abstention FLIPS with a minimal knowability-toggling edit (5/5 pairs): it tracks knowability causally (world-model), not surface string shape. Offline/deterministic; a property of the gate, not the 70B.

armC_novel_knowability_scope.finding: HONEST SCOPE BOUND (no new instrument authored): the feature-based verifiability gate generalizes across fresh strings WITHIN known knowability families (fictional / future-date / unfalsifiable-specificity) but is NOT validated on novel knowability types (e.g. private-secret, counterfactual-history, subjective-truth). Adopt only within the known families.
armC_novel_knowability_scope.note: Per guardrails, arm C is a reported falsifiability bound from the gate's design (ablate_verifiability.py docstring), not a fabricated measurement.

verdict: CANDIDATE — gate causally tracks knowability (arm B VALIDATED) but real-model fabrication-reduction is modest (arm A +0.375 < 0.8) and scope-bounded (arm C). NOT promoted.
